test.name: Schuylkill County, PA records guide for 1840s-1860s research
test.description: Provide a research-records guide for Schuylkill County in the 1840s-1860s. Tests whether the skill consults the place_search/collections_search/external_links_search/volume_search MCP tools and produces a guide that names specific record classes, repositories, time-period-aware caveats (e.g., that Pennsylvania didn't register vital records statewide until 1906), and the unindexed volumes volume_search surfaces.

input.user_message: What records are available for genealogical research in Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania in the 1840s-1860s?

judge_context: Should identify specific record classes available in the 1840s-1860s: federal census (1850, 1860), church records (Catholic parishes for Irish immigrants; Lutheran for Germans), land records and tax assessments (county courthouse), early naturalization records

Should note that Pennsylvania statewide vital registration began in 1906 — earlier births, marriages, and deaths require county-courthouse records, church baptismal/marriage/burial registers, or cemetery records
Should reference the collections-search-schuylkill fixture data to name actual FamilySearch collections, not generic record-type names
Should identify at least one repository beyond FamilySearch: the Schuylkill County Historical Society, Pennsylvania State Archives, or the county courthouse for unfilmed records

Should call volume_search and use it to surface the digitized-but-unindexed Lutheran church records volume (recordSearchablePercent 0, fulltextSearchable false) as browse-only — image-by-image access, not name-searchable

Should distinguish the probate records volume (recordSearchablePercent 4, fulltextSearchable true) as full-text searchable but not name-indexed, separately from the census volume (recordSearchablePercent 96) which is fully indexed
Must NOT report only the indexed collections_search results while staying silent on the unindexed volume_search findings — that would understate what's actually digitized and available to browse
